nodes.label: AI as debugging assistant
nodes.label_negative: AI as unreliable or unhelpful in debugging
nodes.grounding_span: I use cursor to try and first identify what part of the JSON is causing the patient to output a certain value

nodes.label: AI code change validity and logical soundness
nodes.label_negative: AI code changes that are incorrect or illogical
nodes.grounding_span: making sure its not making changes that just so happen to work

nodes.label: AI as data analysis assistant for pattern identification
nodes.grounding_span: I prefer to recognize the trends themself and then tell cursor

nodes.label: AI reliability and hallucination
nodes.grounding_span: it's not a completely dependable tool, but as if you have someone in your ear saying, 'Have you thought about it this way?'

nodes.label: Professional efficiency
nodes.grounding_span: I am now excelling at my work

nodes.label: Satisfaction with AI for debugging
nodes.grounding_span: Many times I have felt satisfied when it helps me debug things that others have struggled with

nodes.label: Frustration with AI errors
nodes.grounding_span: I am most frustrated when it keeps telling me incorrect things about my codebase

nodes.label: Distrust due to hallucination experience
nodes.grounding_span: People tend to be hesitant around hallucination, but this is something that shouldn't deter use

nodes.label: Hands-on troubleshooting and iterative testing
nodes.grounding_span: I cycle through running the patient until I have figured out what the core issue is

nodes.label: Proactive leadership and skill adaptation
nodes.grounding_span: I am starting to lead initiatives for this to improve efficiency
